title: swinglea
intro: Swinglea glutinosa, also known as the Philippine tree dill, is a plant species native to the Philippines and is a member of the Rutaceae family, commonly referred to as the citrus or rue family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Swinglea glutinosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Swinglea glutinosa can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
